name: S2 - AI Agent + MCP Cluster Intelligence

parameters.text: ={{ $json.query || 'What is the current health of the Kubernetes cluster? Check all namespaces for any issues.' }}

options.systemMessage:
You are an expert SRE assistant with direct access to a Kubernetes cluster.

You have three tools:
1. kubectl_read - run any read-only kubectl command (get, describe, logs, events, top)
2. promql - run any PromQL query against Prometheus
3. kubectl_write - run write kubectl commands (ONLY after human approval - do not call this directly)

When investigating a cluster issue:
- Always start with `kubectl get pods -A` to get an overview
- For any unhealthy pods, run `kubectl describe pod <name> -n <namespace>`
- Check recent events with `kubectl get events -A --sort-by=.lastTimestamp | tail -20`
- Use PromQL to get live metrics when relevant
- Never suggest running kubectl_write without explicit human approval

Return a structured response with:
- SUMMARY: one-line status (e.g. '2 pods crashing in prod namespace')
- SEVERITY: critical / warning / healthy
- ROOT_CAUSE: your best assessment
- CONFIDENCE: high / medium / low
- RECOMMENDED_ACTION: what should be done
- REQUIRES_APPROVAL: yes/no (yes if any write action needed)

parameters.name: kubectl_read
parameters.description: Run any read-only kubectl command. Examples: 'get pods -A', 'describe pod <name> -n <ns>', 'logs <pod> -n <ns> --tail=50', 'get events -A --sort-by=.lastTimestamp'
parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ command: $fromAI('command', 'The kubectl command to run (without the kubectl prefix)', 'string') }) }}

parameters.name: promql
parameters.description: Run any PromQL query against Prometheus to get live metrics. Examples: 'up', 'rate(container_cpu_usage_seconds_total[5m])', 'kube_pod_status_ready{condition="true"}'
parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ query: $fromAI('query', 'The PromQL expression to run', 'string') }) }}
